items.title: House price forecasting
items.scenario: A real-estate platform has 200,000 past sales with features (sq ft, bedrooms, location) and label y = sale price in dollars. The product needs a dollar estimate for new listings, not just "expensive vs cheap."
items.explanation: Regression — the target is continuous currency. MAE/RMSE on held-out prices measure success; a classifier on price bins would discard the exact amount users need.

items.title: Email spam detection
items.scenario: Each training email is labeled spam or ham. The mail server must block or allow mail — a binary decision with no meaningful numeric distance between classes.
items.explanation: Binary classification — two discrete labels. Logistic regression or another classifier outputs P(spam|x); precision/recall matter more than a fake numeric "spam score" treated as regression.

items.title: MNIST digit recognition
items.scenario: Images of handwritten digits 0–9, one correct digit per image. The model must output exactly one class among ten mutually exclusive options.
items.explanation: Multi-class classification — softmax over 10 classes with cross-entropy loss. Treating digit labels 0–9 as a regression target implies that digit 8 is "twice" digit 4, which is meaningless.

items.title: Customer churn risk score vs churn label
items.scenario: A telecom has historical accounts labeled churned (1) or retained (0). Product A shows a 0–100% risk gauge; Product B only triggers a retention offer when churn is predicted.
items.explanation: The underlying problem is classification (churn yes/no). Product A exposes the model's calibrated probability; Product B applies a threshold on that probability. The score is not regression on a continuous "churn amount."

items.title: Temperature forecasting
items.scenario: Weather stations record features (pressure, humidity, wind) and next-day high temperature in °C. Operations need a numeric forecast for energy load planning.
items.explanation: Regression on a continuous physical quantity. Rounding to integer degrees for display is formatting, not a reason to switch to 200-class classification.
